content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.5-xhigh

## Summary

Found two fix-now correctness issues in the classification hand-off: stale final-review voting guidance about restart behavior, and review-stage follow-up loss when classified and legacy fields are mixed.

## Amendments

1. fix-now medium: Final-review voter and arbiter prompts still tell reviewers that a mapped planned-elsewhere/covered-by amendment will not trigger a restart, but the current implementation deliberately treats every classification as restart-triggering via ReviewFindingClass::triggers_restart() and needs_restart. This can bias reviewers into accepting amendments under false routing semantics. Affected lines: src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2539, src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2545, src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2586, src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2592, src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2088, src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:44. Remediation: update the voter/arbiter note to say classification is preserved for downstream routing but currently remains fix-now-equivalent/restart-triggering, or remove the no-restart note until the later routing bead lands.
   Rationale: This is within the current contract-addition bead because it is the prompt hand-off for the new classification field and directly contradicts the bead's scope guard that behavior must remain unchanged for now.
2. fix-now medium: Review-stage remediation drops legacy follow_up_or_amendments whenever classified_findings is non-empty. validation_follow_ups() switches entirely to classified_findings at src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:8196 and maps only that list at src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:8204, while the review contract still requires follow_up_or_amendments for non-approved outcomes at src/contexts/workflow_composition/contracts.rs:230. A payload containing classified metadata for one item plus additional legacy follow-ups will silently queue only the classified subset, changing existing default behavior and losing requested fixes. Remediation: either validate that classified_findings fully covers follow_up_or_amendments, or merge any unclassified follow_up_or_amendments not represented in classified_findings as FixCurrentBead before building QueuedAmendment records.
   Rationale: The bead explicitly requires backward-compatible default behavior and propagation to amendment records; silently discarding legacy follow-ups violates that behavior when mixed payloads appear.
